title: senna procumbens
intro: Senna procumbens, a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States, is a member of the legume family and is also known as wild senna or senna shrub. The medicinal properties of senna procumbens lie in its ability to act as a laxative and emetic, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and induce vomiting when ingested in large quantities. The health benefits of senna procumbens include its use in treating constipation, indigestion, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Senna procumbens can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to treat digestive issues or externally as a topical treatment for skin conditions.
